notes: Machine-readable index of every dataset in this mod. Three power-generation tiers organised by drilling depth: Geothermal Well 2.5 m (~12 kW, shallow, water-driven, any biome), Deep Borehole Geothermal 5 m (~60 kW, EGS-style hot-dry-rock, water-driven, any biome — the mid-tier option for players without access to volcanic biomes), and Magma Tap 5 m (~200 kW, volcanic-biome-locked, produces obsidian as byproduct). Introduces lava (new fluid, location-bound in v0.1 — recorded as a fluid record so pumpability can be added later without migration) and obsidian (new raw material, forward-hooks Stone & Concrete's Cement and a hypothetical future Glass mod). SECOND MOD WITH HARD CROSS-MOD ID REFERENCES (after Chemistry & Plastics): the Magma Tap's buildComponents include `composite_plastic` from Chemistry & Plastics for heat-resistant composite casings — the validator walks the Chemistry & Plastics repo via CHEMISTRY_PLASTICS_REPO env var with the same graceful-skip pattern. The Deep Borehole deliberately uses only base-game materials to keep composite_plastic as the signal that a block is rated for lava contact. NEW PRECEDENT FOR THE VALIDATOR: also loads base-game fluids.json + gases.json into the known-ids set so that fuel.consumesId can reference `water` (previous mod validators didn't need to because no mod yet had declared a base-game fluid as a fuel).

datasets.description: Obsidian — new volcanic-glass raw material produced by the Magma Tap 5 m and found naturally in volcanic biomes.

Lava — new environmental fluid. v0.1 is location-bound (consumed in place by the Magma Tap, not routed through conveyors); the record is shaped as a fluid so pumpability can be enabled later without a schema migration.

Three power-generation tiers: Geothermal Well 2.5 m (shallow, ~12 kW, any biome), Deep Borehole Geothermal 5 m (EGS-style, ~60 kW, any biome), and Magma Tap 5 m (volcanic-biome-locked, ~200 kW, obsidian byproduct).
